review_summary.college_name: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Computer Application (BCA) - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Absolutely dissatisfied because of the faculty, despite good placements in all previous years.

content.Placements: It provides very good placements and good education. The minimum package for BCA students is 3 to 15 LPA. Recruiting companies are TCS, Wipro, and the best companies.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure is very good. A very good ground for playing and doing other activities. In this college there are many departments like the library, auditorium, classroom, etc. There are many departments in this college.
content.Faculty: Faculty are very educated and well behaved, and qualified. Semester exams are very easy, do not worry about it. Pass percentage is only 40%.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: Satisfied because of faculty and good infrastructure.

content.Placements: 60-70% students are placed in my course. Packages offered in my course are Rs 25,000 to Rs 30,000. The college gives different packages according to the hostel rooms, etc. Top recruiting companies are TATA, finance companies, and other smaller companies that come to the college every year. 30-40% students get internships in finance and banking companies.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi is available, classrooms are good, the library is very big and has good books, labs are also there for science students. Average quality facilities are available in the canteen and others. Sports and games are conducted in this college every year, which is good.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com. Hons.) - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: It's Not a Good College for Your Dream College Life

content.Placements: 50% is enough for admission in this course at my college. But the fee structure is so bad because they bargain like in a fish market. In my personal opinion, if you really want to do something in your life, please don't take any course from there; they just spoil your college life.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi is not available, classrooms are always dirty, or sometimes we see strange things in our classrooms like condoms. The library is always filled with troublemakers; it’s not safe for girls, which is why no girls go there to study.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Review of Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions.

content.Placements: More than 90% of students were selected by companies. For the 2018-2022 batch, the highest salary package offered was 8 LPA and the lowest salary package offered was 2.5 LPA. In our course, top recruiting companies are TCS, Wipro, Infosys, Protecons, etc. Many internships were offered depending on students.
content.Infrastructure: We had Wi-Fi available in computer labs for practicals. CCTVs were installed in classrooms and throughout the infrastructure. Facilities of the hostel and college were good. The food served at the canteen and mess was good. In the Sidhi Vinayak Group of Institutions, there was a library on the second floor with many books.

individual_reviews.course: B.Tech. in Information Technology - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: It is good in terms of infrastructure, facilities, and placements.

content.Placements: Around 80% of students get placed in different companies. The highest salary package offered is 7 LPA, and the average is 3.6 LPA. Some of the top recruiting companies are TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Collabera, etc. One student from our course got placed in four IT companies. The top role offered is that of an IT engineer in many service-based companies.
content.Infrastructure: For B.Tech courses, there is a separate building in our college. There are good facilities for students, including a hostel, a workshop, and free Wi-Fi, along with computer labs and a huge library for students. The quality of the food in the mess and canteen is good.

admission_overview.college_name: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions Course Admissions 2025: Fees, Eligibility, Dates & Application Process

admission_overview.title: SSVGI Admission Overview 2025
admission_overview.description: SSVGI admission 2025 is done on the basis of both academic merit and entrance exam scores, depending on the course and category. The institute offers a wide range of courses in the fields of Engineering, Management, Computer Applications, Commerce, Nursing, Science, and more. The college accepts national-level entrance exams, such as CAT/ UPSEE/ CMAT/ JEE, etc., for admission to its popular courses BTech and MBA. Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions accepts applications online. The courses are offered via SSVGI constituent schools, namely BCA, BSc, BCom, BCom (H), GNM, ANM, Dpharma, etc. The institute is affliated to AKTU Lucknow, BTE Lucknow, MJPRU Lucknow, and All India Paramedical Council Lucknow, and UPSMF. Interested students can read the following article to learn all about the courses, eligibility, selection criteria, and admission procedure for Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions.

eligibility_selection.title: SSVGI Eligibility and Selection Criteria 2025
eligibility_selection.description: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions course admission is subject to the fulfilment of the preset admission criteria for the same. Those seeking to join the institute need to do so by completing the admission process. Candidates seeking admission to SSVGI courses are required to meet the eligibility criteria of their desired course in order to apply for admission to the programme. For certain programmes, the institution accepts valid scores secured in the national-level or state-level entrance tests, whereas, merit-based admissions are also accepted. Further, students can also apply via the management quota. The basic eligibility criteria for SSVGI UG and PG courses are as follows:

courses_table.eligibility: Class 12 in the Science Stream from a recognised board
Graduation in any stream with a minimum aggregate of 50% from a recognised university
Class 12 in PCM from a recognised board
BTech or equivalent with a minimum aggregate of 60% from a recognised university

Class 12 with PCB/PCM + English or Arts (Mathematics, Biotechnology, Economics, Political Science, History, Geography, Business Studies, Accountancy, Home Science, Sociology, Psychology, Philosophy, Civics, Education) and English Core/English Elective or Health care

faqs.answer: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions offers a wide range of courses, eligbility to which varies. Student seeking to join the institute are required to meet specified requirements before applying. Aspiring students can refer to the following table to know about the general eligibility criteria of the programmes offered at the institute:CoursesEligibilityBBAClass 12 from a recognised boardBCAClass 12 with Mathematics and a minimum aggergate of 45% from a recognised boardBComClass 12 in Commerce stream and a minimum aggergate of 45%GNMClass 12 with PCB/PCM with English or Arts (Mathematics, Biotechnology, Economics, Political Science, History, Geography, Business Studies, Accountancy, Home Science, Sociology, Psychology, Philosophy, Civics, Education) and English Core/English Elective or Health care. Science Vocational streamB.E. / BTechCandidate must have passed Diploma in relevant stream from a recognised Institute.BScCandidate must have passed 10+2 with PCM with mentioned marks from a recognised board.M.E./MTechMSc (Computer Science, IT) or (MCA) with 60% in aggregate (55% for SC/ST) are also eligible to apply.MBA/PGDMGraduation from a recognised university with a minimum aggregate of 50%Candidate who are appearing for final exam can also apply.
faqs.question: What is the eligibility to get into Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions?

faqs.answer: This usually changes every year, as more applications pour in, and different levels of performance and seats available emerge in the MBA courses within the Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions (SSVGI). Normally, students who are scoring around 500-600 in MAT can hope for admission into the colleges. Prospective students should cross-check on the official SSVGI website or contact the admissions office for the current and specific cutoff details since they may change with different admissions cycles and programme demands.
faqs.question: What is the MAT cutoff for Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions?

paragraphs: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions admission processinvolves multiple steps. Selection is based on both, merit and entrance exams. The process is mainly conducted online. The admission criteria for all undergraduate and postgraduate courses, except BTech and MBA, are merit-based. For BTech, the institute accepts entrance exam scores, and for MBA admission, candidates must have a valid CAT/ CMAT/ XAT/ MAT scorecard. Those interested can check out the completeSSVGI admission processexplained below:
1 - Check the Eligibility

Aspirants must first check the eligibility criteria of the course they wish to pursue. The official website of the institute has detailed admission criteria for each of the offered programmes. The basic gist is given in the section above for various SSVGI courses.
2 - Application
The college accepts both online and offline admissions. Online applications can be made by visiting the official website.
3 - Selection rounds

Candidates need to appear for the relevant entrance exam (if required) to get shortlisted for admission. The selection criteria for almost all courses are merit-based. For admission to courses, such as BTech, MBA/ PGDM, MTech, etc., scores secured in entrance tests are considered. Similarly, for admissions to other courses, students need to provide the merit secured in the last qualifying exam.
4 - Admission confirmation
Once the selection rounds are done, the shortlisted candidates are called for document verification. Aspirants need to pay theSSVGI feesto confirm their seat in the college.
Download SSGVI Brochure

faqs.answer: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions has courses such as BBA, BCA, B.Com, ANM, GNM, B.Tech, B.Sc., After 1th Diploma, M.E./M.Tech, MBA/PGDM which are primarily in the stream of Business & Management Studies, IT & Software, Accounting & Commerce, Nursing, Engineering, Science. faqs.question: Can Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions be considered a good college?
faqs.answer: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions is a reputed institute for BBA, BCA, B.Com, ANM, GNM, B.Tech, B.Sc., After 1th Diploma, M.E./M.Tech, MBA/PGDM. Students can opt these programs to pursue their career in Business & Management Studies, IT & Software, Accounting & Commerce, Nursing, Engineering, Science. Institute has trained & experienced faculty who deliver these programs. Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions has a review rating of 3.8 on the basis of reviews by students. In addition to this, it has been rated 3.7 on campuslife,  3.3 on placements,  4.2 on faculty and 4.1 on Infrastructure.

description: Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions cutoff 2025 has been announced for admission to BTech programme for all categories and rounds in the both the All India and Home State quota. Students can scroll to the exam-wise sections below for details about Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions UPTAC Cutoff 2025 and other exams. A few more exam-related updates are in the pointers below:
A few more updates are given below:

The Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions cutoff 2024 was concluded for JEE Main with the release of the final cutoff list. The cutoff was released as the opening and closing ranks for admission to the BE/BTech, courses. During the Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions BE/BTech cutoff 2024, the last-round closing rank was 997642 for the General AI category students. Of all the BE/BTech specialisations, the one that faced fierce competition was B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering.

The JEE Main cutoff 2024 was released for the General, OBC, ST, SC, EWS, and PWD categories in the AI quota. Considering the last round 2024 cutoff, B.Tech. in Computer Science and Engineering was the most sought-after BE/BTech specialization with a closing rank of 997642 for the general AI quota. The closing cutoff for the same stood at 1018197 in 2023.

Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions accepts JEE Main scores, followed by JoSAA and CSAB counselling rounds for admission to BTech and Integrated BTech courses. Candidates seeking admission to Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions can refer to the table below for tentative JEE Main schedule.

Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions JEE Main cutoff 2024 is now over for BTech, Integrated BTech, and BArch courses. For JEE Main, a total of five counselling rounds were conducted. Additionally, two CSAB counselling rounds were also conducted to fill the vacant seats. Candidates can refer to the table below to download the consolidated year-wise counselling rounds for Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions.

As the Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions UPTAC Cutoff 2025 has been concluded for all rounds and categories under the AI and HS quota. In this section, students can see UPTAC Cutoff 2025 for admission to various B.E. / B.Tech specialisations.

For 2025, in the last round, the top three most competitive specialisations for admission in the General AI category students were B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering, followed by B.Tech. in Information Technology, and B.Tech. in Civil Engineering, with the closing ranks of 2112266, 2117562 and 2119335, respectively.

For admission to Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions for B.E. / B.Tech students have to clear the UPTAC exam and attain valid ranks to qualify for a seat at this institute. Students can see the table below to know the important dates for admission to Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely dissatisfied because of the faculty, despite good placements in all previous years.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is very good. A very good ground for playing and doing other activities. In this college there are many departments like the library, auditorium, classroom, etc. There are many departments in this college.

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied because of faculty and good infrastructure.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is available, classrooms are good, the library is very big and has good books, labs are also there for science students. Average quality facilities are available in the canteen and others. Sports and games are conducted in this college every year, which is good.

reviews.review_heading: It's Not a Good College for Your Dream College Life
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is not available, classrooms are always dirty, or sometimes we see strange things in our classrooms like condoms. The library is always filled with troublemakers; it’s not safe for girls, which is why no girls go there to study.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions.
reviews.review_text: We had Wi-Fi available in computer labs for practicals. CCTVs were installed in classrooms and throughout the infrastructure. Facilities of the hostel and college were good. The food served at the canteen and mess was good. In the Sidhi Vinayak Group of Institutions, there was a library on the second floor with many books.

reviews.review_heading: It is good in terms of infrastructure, facilities, and placements.
reviews.review_text: For B.Tech courses, there is a separate building in our college. There are good facilities for students, including a hostel, a workshop, and free Wi-Fi, along with computer labs and a huge library for students. The quality of the food in the mess and canteen is good.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied with the good placement of 2022 batch, really good faculty.
reviews.review_text: My college is widely spread in near nainital road dohna. It has got a large number of computer labs available and free Wi-Fi facilities up to a limit and decent classrooms for students. In the hostel there are many facilities with good food quality and menu of mess and canteen. It has ground for different sports.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely average college with average placements and faculty but really good infrastructure.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is not available in this college. Your phone doesn't get signals because of jammers placed at this college. The library and classrooms are good, but you may get problems in heavy summer conditions due to a lack of coolers. The quality of food in the canteen is average. You may not like to eat more than 2 times a week.

reviews.review_heading: Seniors are very helpful and supportive, and they will help you in every moment.
reviews.review_text: In this course, we get to learn a lot. BCA provides good skills and information not only about computers and information technology but also about communication. The infrastructure and facilities like classrooms, smart libraries, Wi-Fi, and hostel are good and well-maintained. The living spaces are clean, and the food is hygienic.

reviews.review_heading: The college is good but not the best.
reviews.review_text: All the facilities are available, but all are average. The libraries are outdated and the labs have good systems but most of the time they are used by the TCSION, canteen is the worst and unhealthy. In campus, they have only boys hostel and for girls, they have a PG in the city.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions.
reviews.review_text: It is a clean and good campus. The library has many books to read and also you can borrow the books from the students for 5 days to read books. Facilities are good, and the medical facility is also good for everyone.

reviews.review_heading: Good College you can go. Good staff. And nice environment.
reviews.review_text: College infrastructure is too good best college in Bareilly in view of infrastructure and development all the classes are new have proper ventilation system even bathroom are good and have Wi-Fi all around campus .

reviews.review_heading: In SSVIT, there is a really good infrastructure and facilities and knowledgeable teachers.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the college is very good. There are many libraries with computers and books. In this college, a Wi-Fi facility is available for students, which helps to find something better in the study. There is a canteen for students and faculty members for lunch. In the hostel also, there is a mess canteen.

reviews.review_heading: I completed my B.Tech in 2015, and the college has great infrastructure and facilities.
reviews.review_text: Our college has great infrastructure with the best labs, canteen, library, and most importantly, free Wi-Fi. The hostel has a great atmosphere. Food is good in the hostel. Every weekend, curricular activities are conducted such as cricket, singing etc.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Shri Siddhi Vinayak Group of Institutions.
reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure available for our course are good and comfortable. A huge library, canteen, smart classrooms, science labs, etc., are available. I don't stay in the hostel, so I don't know about the quality of facilities provided in our hostel. The food available in our canteen is very tasty and healthy. faqs.question: What is the eligibility for taking admission in BPharma programme at Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy?

faqs.answer: Candidates applying for MPharma programme at Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy should have completed BPharma degree from PCI and AICTE approved institute with a minimum of 60% marks for General/OBC and 45% for reserved category students.
faqs.question: What is the eligibility for taking admission in MPharma programme at Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy?

faqs.answer: No,  Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy generally admits BPharma graduates  from PCI and AICTE approved institute with a minimum of 60% marks for General/OBC and 45% for reserved category students for M.Tech programme. So BSc graduate cannot take admission in MPharma.

paragraphs: Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy admission2026 is entrance-based. The college accepts score of entrance exam such as JEECUP, CUET UG and CUET PG for Diploma, UG and PG courses respectively. Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy offers three courses in the field of pharmacy.

The duration of Diploma course is two-year and for UG courses, it is four years. For PG programmes, the duration is two years. Candidates can check below the detailed steps of admission process at Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy:
Application Process

A candidate has to fill the application form of college from the official website of college. They can submit it either online or offline. Candidate slso need to register for the respective entrance exam required for admission into UG, PG or Diploma programme. Candidates must fill the online application form, pay the fee, and save the form in PDF.
Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy Result/Counselling

The deemed-to-be university releases results as per the schedule and based on the merit obtained in the entrance exam and subject to meeting the minimum eligibility conditions. After the results, allocates seats in different programmes after JEECUP and CUET counselling.
Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy Final Admission
The candidate will be provisionally admitted to the programme after paying the course-wise tuition fees. This helps the student to secure a seat in their prefferd course.

Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Pharmacy total tuition fee vary depending on the programme. It is composed of different components. The institute also offers multiple seats for its courses. reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: No good placements visits for B. Pharma.

review_content.Placements: No placements for the degree; very few students get placements. The offered package was between 3-4 LPA. Companies like Glenmark. Only 2% of students were placed among 126 students in the college. The company rarely comes to the college for placements. Very bad condition.
review_content.Infrastructure: Overall infrastructure is good, with Wi-Fi available throughout the environment. Washrooms are satisfactory, benches are nice, and there is no ragging on the campus. reviews_list.review_title: Our college provides good education.

review_content.Placements: About 90% of students got placed. The highest package offered in the college is 30,000 per month, and the lowest package offered in the college is 10,000. Students got placed in the top companies. The college provides opportunities for students to get placed in top companies.
review_content.Infrastructure: Computer labs, Wi-Fi, practical labs and a library are available. Buses are available. Hostel facilities are available. review_summary.college_name: Matoshri College of Pharmacy Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: Nice college, average students, and I love my college as lectures are taken and campus is huge

content.Placements: The placement is average, and the package provided is 2 LPA for B.Pharma and 4 LPA for pharma students. Few students get selected. I recommend that placement is below average. In Nashik, there are many colleges where placement is better. I think this by my choice.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure is well and good, I say, but it is small in front of other colleges. The hostel is good and all facilities are available. Mess is available. I live in the hostel, and everything is proper as my experience says it all. The campus is huge and bigger. I love it.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: Good faculty members are available here.

content.Placements: Almost 95% of students get placed here. The lowest salary package offered in the college is 3 LPA and the highest package salary package offered is 12 LPA. Various companies visit our college for placements, and they offer important roles for the students.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi is available in our college. The Wi-Fi speed is very high. Practical labs are very clean in our college, and the practical labs have certain equipment. The library of our college is very large, and it has each subject book.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: No good placements visits for B. Pharma.

content.Placements: No placements for the degree; very few students get placements. The offered package was between 3-4 LPA. Companies like Glenmark. Only 2% of students were placed among 126 students in the college. The company rarely comes to the college for placements. Very bad condition.
content.Infrastructure: Overall infrastructure is good, with Wi-Fi available throughout the environment. Washrooms are satisfactory, benches are nice, and there is no ragging on the campus. All staff are cooperative. The library is available, and there are separate labs. The canteen offers mess options or breakfast, along with basic Indian snacks.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Our college provides good education.

content.Placements: About 90% of students got placed. The highest package offered in the college is 30,000 per month, and the lowest package offered in the college is 10,000. Students got placed in the top companies. The college provides opportunities for students to get placed in top companies.
content.Infrastructure: Computer labs, Wi-Fi, practical labs and a library are available. Buses are available. Hostel facilities are available. They take care of all the students in the hostel, and they pay personal attention to students' problems. Various events are held in the college regarding sports.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharma.) - Batch of 2020
individual_reviews.title: Overall, it is an average college.

content.Placements: Due to the situation of COVID-19 pandemic, companies were not able to visit the college for placements. However, about 40% of the students got jobs now, while 50% of the students opt for further studies. The top role offered is the executive manager. Our batch has the most talented students than others. In our batch, 99.99% is the passing percentage.
content.Infrastructure: Facilities are not good according to college fee. However, classrooms, library and labs are good. I stay in a private hostel and not in our college hostel. The private hostel is better than the college hostel as per my experience. So, I prefer a private hostel to have a good and healthy life and food.

admission_overview.college_name: Matoshri College of Pharmacy Admission 2025: Fees, Courses, Eligibility and Selection

admission_process.title: Matoshri College of Pharmacy Admission Process 2025

paragraphs: Matoshri College of Pharmacy admissions are based on merit. The institute considers applications of candidates meeting the eligibility criteria.Matoshri College of Pharmacyoffers courses at UG and PG levels. Some of the courses include B.Pharma, D.Pharma and M.Pharma. Candidates can apply online for the preferred course.
The total tuition fees for UG courses isINR 12,000 - 3,21,836. Aspirants may also need to pay one-time caution deposit at the time of admission.
The institute offers UG courses for 2 years - 4 years. PG courses are offered for 2 years.

courses_data.name: Matoshri College of Pharmacy B.Pharma Admission 2025
courses_data.description: Matoshri College of Pharmacy B.Pharmais offered to candidates at the UG level. Candidates are required to participate in the entrance-based admission process to be considered for the final admission offer. Candidates are shortlisted for the B.Pharma programme based on their performance in the entrance exam. Candidates who qualify in the admission process are eligible to secure their seat in the 4 years programme.

courses_data.name: Matoshri College of Pharmacy D.Pharma Admission 2025
courses_data.description: TheD.PharmaatMatoshri College of Pharmacyis a 2 years programme offered to the students in the field of Medicine & Health Sciences. Admission to this course is offered based on a valid score in CBSE 12th and Maharashtra HSC. To complete the Matoshri College of Pharmacy admission process for the 2025 batch, selected candidates must pay the applicable course fee ofINR 12,000.
